items.title: 10 Best Open-Source LLM Models (2025 Updated): Llama 4, Qwen 3 and DeepSeek R1 — Hugging Face Blog
items.author: huggingface.co
items.snippet: The open-source LLM landscape in early 2026 is dominated by three major model families: Meta's Llama 4, Alibaba's Qwen 3, and DeepSeek V3.2. Meta released Llama 4 in multiple variants (Scout, Maverick, and the upcoming Behemoth), with Scout offering an unprecedented 10M token context window and strong multimodal capabilities. Qwen 3, notably the 80B Qwen3-Coder-Next model (with only ~3B active parameters via MoE architecture), has drawn significant attention for outperforming much larger models

items.title: Sharing new breakthroughs supporting molecular property prediction, language processing, and neuroscience
items.author: ai.meta.com
items.snippet: Meta's most significant recent open source AI release is Llama 4, announced in early 2026. The Llama 4 herd introduces natively multimodal models built on a mixture-of-experts (MoE) architecture. The two flagship models — Llama 4 Scout and Llama 4 Maverick — support unprecedented context lengths, were pre-trained on over 30 trillion tokens (2x more than Llama 3), and include 200+ languages with 10x more multilingual tokens than the prior generation. Both are available as open weights on llama.co

items.title: OpenAI raises $110B in one of the largest private funding rounds in history
items.author: techcrunch.com
items.snippet: AI startup funding in early 2026 has been historic in scale. OpenAI raised $110 billion in one of the largest private funding rounds ever, drawing $50B from Amazon and $30B each from Nvidia and SoftBank at a $730B pre-money valuation. xAI closed a $20B Series E in January, while Humans& — founded by Anthropic, xAI, and Google alumni — raised a remarkable $480M seed round, signaling extreme investor appetite for foundational AI talent. Defense AI unicorn Harmattan AI raised $200M Series B led by

items.title: Multi-Agent LLMs for Adaptive Acquisition in Bayesian Optimization
items.author: arxiv.org
items.snippet: This week's arXiv activity shows a strong focus on agentic AI systems across multiple domains. A major survey paper (2503.21460) by Luo et al. offers a methodology-centered taxonomy of LLM agent architectures, covering collaboration mechanisms, evolutionary pathways, and applications — serving as a comprehensive reference for the field. On the multi-agent side, two notable papers emerged: one studying LLM-agent interactions in markets with information asymmetries using GPT-5.1 simulations (2603.

items.title: Hugging Face – Blog
items.author: huggingface.co
items.snippet: Recent HuggingFace blog posts highlight several notable model releases in early 2026. NVIDIA has been particularly active, releasing Cosmos Reason 2 — an advanced reasoning vision-language model for physical AI that tops the Physical AI Bench and Physical Reasoning leaderboards as the #1 open model for visual understanding. NVIDIA also launched Nemotron 3 Nano, a 30B parameter model using a hybrid Mamba-Transformer Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architecture with a 1M-token context window, alongside s

items.title: awesome-ai-agents-2026 — 300+ AI agent resources
items.author: github.com
items.snippet: As of early April 2026, GitHub's AI agent ecosystem is experiencing explosive growth, with several standout repositories gaining significant weekly stars. Key trending projects include multi-agent orchestration frameworks like OWL (allowing specialized agents to cooperate on tasks), LangChain (recently crossing 100,000 stars), and Langflow (a low-code drag-and-drop platform for RAG and agent workflows). The GitHub Blog highlighted a curated top-10 list of new open-source AI projects spanning MCP
